raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The draft names ATA an 'attention mechanism' but then formulates it entirely as a state-space recurrence (h_t = A·h_{t-1} + B·x_t); the thesis and contribution claim require reconciliation to be coherent.","R1.2":"Multiple internal contradictions are present: the 'learnable gating function over relative time offsets' introduced in paragraph 1 has no counterpart in the SSM equations; paragraph 3 claims the model avoids attention masks while the opening frames it as an attention mechanism; these cross-paragraph incoherences are central improvement targets.","R1.3":"Empirical claims (MSE 0.412 vs 0.439, 6% ablation degradation, 'no further improvement' from dense A) and the unsupported forward-reference to Section 5 for generalization evidence must be checked for alignment with the conclusions drawn.","R1.6":"'Improve this draft' canonically loads writing polish as a primary expectation; sentence clarity, transition quality, and terminological precision are load-bearing regardless of scope."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should improvement address substantive technical issues — such as the mismatch between calling ATA an 'attention mechanism' and formulating it as a state-space recurrence, and the unresolved gating-function claim — or is the scope limited to writing clarity and prose polish?"}

rationale.R1.1: The draft names ATA an 'attention mechanism' but then formulates it entirely as a state-space recurrence (h_t = A·h_{t-1} + B·x_t); the thesis and contribution claim require reconciliation to be coherent.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple internal contradictions are present: the 'learnable gating function over relative time offsets' introduced in paragraph 1 has no counterpart in the SSM equations; paragraph 3 claims the model avoids attention masks while the opening frames it as an attention mechanism; these cross-paragraph incoherences are central improvement targets.
rationale.R1.3: Empirical claims (MSE 0.412 vs 0.439, 6% ablation degradation, 'no further improvement' from dense A) and the unsupported forward-reference to Section 5 for generalization evidence must be checked for alignment with the conclusions drawn.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' canonically loads writing polish as a primary expectation; sentence clarity, transition quality, and terminological precision are load-bearing regardless of scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvement address substantive technical issues — such as the mismatch between calling ATA an 'attention mechanism' and formulating it as a state-space recurrence, and the unresolved gating-function claim — or is the scope limited to writing clarity and prose polish?
